Paper Title: Whole-tree water balance and indicators for short-term drought stress in non-bearing ‘Barnea’ olives
Study Population / Unit of Analysis: Single 2-year old 'Barnea' olive tree (Olea europaea cv Barnea) planted in fifteen 2.5 m^3 volume free-standing lysimeters
Geographical Setting (n): Gilat Research Center, northwestern Negev, Israel
Independent Variable Unit: stem water potential (Ψstem) (-3.5 to -1.3 | MPa); stomatal conductance (gs) (30 to 150 | mmol m^-2 s^-1); quantum yield of photosystem II photochemistry (ΦPSII) (0.2 to 0.6 | not applicable); canopy temperature (Tc) (30 to 36 | °C); crop water stress index (CWSI) (0.2 to 0.6 | not applicable)
